query: I fit an OLS regression on n = 80 observations with p = 3 predictors. Cook's D for observation 42 is 1.4. All other observations have Cook's D below 0.05. Should I just drop observation 42 and refit?
expected_behavior: Names Cook's D > 1 as highly influential and refuses to silently drop observation 42 — the default response is to investigate the observation and report both regressions (with and without the point), not removal as the first action
Recommends inspecting the observation for a substantive reason (data-entry error, out-of-scope case, different population) before any drop decision, and names that 'silently drop' is the anti-pattern
Recommends a side-by-side comparison of fitted coefficients with and without observation 42 so the reader can see which coefficients move and by how much
